place: Cookstown CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Cookstown earned its name when William Cooke bought the 1721 grist mill in 1776, turning the village into a milling hub.
New Hanover Township, where Cookstown sits, was carved out of Springfield and Chesterfield in 1673 by King George II.
Early farmers in Cookstown hauled produce eastward on the Pemberton and Hightstown Railroad, linking the village to regional markets.
While most of Cookstown lies in New Hanover, a slice of the village spills into neighboring North Hanover Township.
Cookstown preserves history with the General Godfrey House on Main Street and a renovated one‑room schoolhouse on Browns Mills‑Cookstown Road.
